risks.evidence: Full catalog table read has no LIMIT/OFFSET.
risks.rootCauseSummary: Primary: src/modules/products/products.service.js:4-7 performs an active-products SELECT ordered by created_at with no LIMIT/OFFSET; src/modules/products/products.controller.js:2 returns the full array and pagination:null.
risks.recommendedFix: Add bounded cursor/keyset pagination (stable created_at,id cursor), a default and maximum page size, and page metadata; preserve an explicit migration path for current clients.

risks.evidence: The former general cart-rows endpoint was replaced by a user-scoped bounded cart resource.
risks.rootCauseSummary: Fixed and not reproduced: src/modules/cart/cart.service.js:3 scopes the cart query to the authenticated user's single cart; no general cross-user collection is exposed.
risks.recommendedFix: No current code change; retain JWT user scoping and inventory-bounded cart semantics and watch for regression to a general cart-list contract.
risks.reviewerStatus: Verified fixed; APIPERF-2 remains Done and was not reopened

risks.evidence: All reviews are returned with no record or response-size bound.
risks.rootCauseSummary: Primary: src/modules/reviews/reviews.service.js:3-5 returns every matching review without LIMIT/OFFSET. Supporting Payload Size cause: reviews.controller.js:2 serializes all rows and explicitly reports pagination:null.
risks.recommendedFix: Add cursor pagination on (created_at,id), default and maximum page sizes, and bounded response metadata; keep APIPERF-1 as the deduplicated unresolved ticket.
risks.reviewerStatus: Verified current; existing unresolved ticket reused

risks.evidence: A separate order_items query is executed for each order.
risks.rootCauseSummary: Primary: src/modules/orders/orders.service.js:2-6 runs one order_items query inside a loop for every order. Supporting causes: the initial order query and controller response have no page/size bound.
risks.recommendedFix: Replace per-order queries with a set-based join/JSON aggregation or two batched queries, and add bounded keyset pagination to the order history.

risks.evidence: High-volume detail reads bypass the configured Redis cache.
risks.rootCauseSummary: Primary: src/modules/products/products.service.js:9-13 queries PostgreSQL directly for every detail request while src/lib/redis.js configures an available Redis client that the product-detail path never uses.
risks.recommendedFix: Implement cache-aside reads keyed by product ID with bounded TTL, product-mutation invalidation, and a tested Redis-degraded fallback.
risks.reviewerStatus: Verified current; improvement task created during review

risks.evidence: Stripe PaymentIntent capture blocks request completion.

manualReviewerNote.author: Priya N., Senior Commerce Engineer
manualReviewerNote.text: confirmed with payments team, latency spike is expected during Diwali sale

risks.rootCauseSummary: Primary: src/modules/checkout/checkout.service.js:2-5 awaits stripe.paymentIntents.create in the HTTP request before updating the order and returning; the manual accepted-risk note is preserved unchanged.
risks.recommendedFix: Persist an idempotent attempt and move PaymentIntent capture to the existing BullMQ worker/webhook flow; if synchronous confirmation is mandatory, enforce a strict timeout, circuit breaker, and reconciliation.
risks.reviewerStatus: Verified current; accepted-risk note preserved; new Jira task created
